usage.description: Descriptive GIS context only. These fields are not used as adaptation-gap score inputs.

sources.name: United Nations Statistics Division M49 standard country or area codes and geographic regions
sources.caveats: Used for Pacific subregion labels and UN short-form country or area names. UNSD states that grouping assignments are for statistical convenience and do not imply political affiliation or legal status.

sources.name: U.S. Department of the Interior Office of Insular Affairs - American Samoa
sources.caveats: Used for high-level U.S. territory context. Local constitutional and citizenship details are summarized and should be reviewed before publication.

sources.name: U.S. Department of the Interior Office of Insular Affairs - Guam
sources.caveats: Used for high-level U.S. territory context. The table does not attempt a full constitutional-law summary.

sources.name: U.S. Department of the Interior Office of Insular Affairs - Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands
sources.caveats: Used for high-level U.S. commonwealth territory context. The table does not attempt a full covenant-law summary.

sources.name: U.S. Department of the Interior Office of Insular Affairs - Federated States of Micronesia
sources.caveats: Used for Compact of Free Association summary. Compact provisions are summarized at a high level only.

sources.name: U.S. Department of the Interior Office of Insular Affairs - The Marshall Islands
sources.caveats: Used for Compact of Free Association summary. Compact renewal details and defense provisions should be checked before publication.

sources.name: U.S. Department of the Interior Office of Insular Affairs - Republic of Palau
sources.caveats: Used for Compact of Free Association summary. Compact provisions are summarized at a high level only.

sources.name: New Zealand Ministry of Foreign Affairs and Trade - Cook Islands
sources.caveats: Used for free-association context. Cook Islands international personality and citizenship relationship are nuanced and flagged for review.

sources.name: New Zealand Ministry of Foreign Affairs and Trade - Niue
sources.caveats: Used for free-association context. Niue international personality and citizenship relationship are nuanced and flagged for review.

sources.name: New Zealand Ministry of Foreign Affairs and Trade - Tokelau
sources.caveats: Used for non-self-governing territory context. Tokelau governance is summarized at a high level only.

sources.name: Legifrance - Constitution of 4 October 1958
sources.caveats: Used for French constitutional context including overseas collectivities and New Caledonia transitional provisions. This table keeps French territory labels high level and flags sensitive cases for review.

sources.name: Legifrance - Organic Law 2004-192 on the autonomy status of French Polynesia
sources.caveats: Used for French Polynesia overseas collectivity and overseas-country wording. UN non-self-governing context should be reviewed separately before publication.

sources.name: Government of the Pitcairn Islands - official website
sources.caveats: Used for British Overseas Territory and island-group context. Governance details are summarized and should be reviewed against UK constitutional sources before publication.

sources.name: U.S. Department of State - Independent States in the World
sources.caveats: Automated access returned a forbidden response during this task. Sovereign-state rows therefore use this as a follow-up review pointer and are marked needs_review_sovereign_status rather than precise legal determinations.

sources.name: Le Monde - State of New Caledonia agreement coverage
sources.caveats: Used only as a current-status review flag for New Caledonia. Not an official legal source. Confirm the latest French and New Caledonian legal position before publication.

artifact_caveats: All rows are descriptive GIS context and must remain outside adaptation-gap scoring unless a future reviewed methodology explicitly changes that.
Pacific subregion labels follow UN M49 statistical groupings and should not be framed as cultural or political boundaries.
Sovereign-state rows use coarse public-status labels and are marked for review because the official country-status source could not be fetched by the automated browser.
Political-status language for free-associated states and non-self-governing or overseas territories is intentionally conservative and should be reviewed before public copy is finalized.
The table does not provide polygon boundaries or settle boundary-source choices.
review_recommendations: Before polygon choropleths, choose and document an authoritative boundary source or keep centroid-only mapping.
Review New Caledonia and French Polynesia labels immediately before publication because status and self-determination context can be politically sensitive.
Use this table for grouping and explanatory context only; do not join it into score calculations.
